info.name: FlatMate App API - v8
info.description:
Complete Postman collection for the FlatMate App API. Covers all modules: Auth (OTP, token, role switching), Profiles (basic, seeker, KYC, owner), Listings (publish, draft, update, delete, upload), Location (geo-index, nearby search), Swipes & Matches, Chat (history, upload, read), and Notifications (FCM token, send, inbox).

**Base URL:** Set {{baseUrl}} to:
- Production: https://hjqqy3z9r1.execute-api.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod
- Local: http://localhost:8081

**Auth Flow:**
1. Send OTP → Verify OTP (get `accessToken` + `refreshToken`) → Set Role → Use Bearer token

**Variables:** Fill in `token`, `refreshToken`, `userId`, `ownerId`, `seekerId`, `listingId`, `matchId` in the collection/environment variables.

variable.description: Production API base URL. Replace with http://localhost:8081 for local testing.
JWT access token from Verify OTP response.
JWT refresh token from Verify OTP response.
Logged-in user's phone number (used as userId).
Phone number of the listing owner.
Phone number of the flat seeker.
ID of a listing to use in listing-specific requests.
ID of a match (used in Chat history).

item.name: Health
item.description: Simple health-check endpoint. No auth required.

request.description: Returns the health status of the API server. Use this to verify the service is up before running other requests.

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Authentication flow using phone-based OTP. Steps:
1. Send OTP to phone
2. Verify OTP to receive tokens
3. (Optional) Refresh access token
4. Set role (first time) or switch role (subsequent times)

After step 2, copy `accessToken` and `refreshToken` into the collection variables.

request.description: Sends a one-time password to the provided phone number. No auth required.

Verifies the OTP. Returns `accessToken`, `refreshToken`, `roles`, `activeRole`, and onboarding flags (`isProfileComplete`, `isKycComplete`, `isRoleSet`).

The test script auto-saves the tokens to collection variables.

exec: console.log('accessToken saved to collection variable: token');
console.log('refreshToken saved to collection variable: refreshToken');

request.description: Refreshes the access token using the refresh token. Use this when the access token has expired. The test script auto-saves the new access token.

Sets the user's role for the first time during onboarding.

Accepted values: `SEEKER`, `OWNER`.
Alias values also accepted: `TENANT` (maps to SEEKER), `HOST` (maps to OWNER).
Switches the active role to SEEKER. User must already have the SEEKER role registered. Useful for users with multiple roles.
Switches the active role to OWNER. User must already have the OWNER role registered.

item.name: Profiles
item.description:
Manage user profiles including basic info, seeker-specific preferences, KYC (document & selfie upload), and owner registration.

All endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`.

request.description: Fetches the full profile for the given userId. Returns basic info, seeker/owner sub-profiles, KYC status, and role history.

Creates or updates the basic profile fields: name, DOB, gender, bio, and profile picture URL.

Gender accepted values: `MAN`, `WOMAN`, `NON_BINARY`, `PREFER_NOT_TO_SAY`.

body.raw:
{
  "userId": "{{userId}}",
  "firstName": "Rajasekar",
  "lastName": "Kumar",
  "dateOfBirth": "1995-06-15",
  "gender": "MAN",
  "bio": "Software engineer looking for a flat near Koramangala.",
  "profilePic": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/flatmate-photos/users/user_001/profile.jpg"
}

request.description:
Updates the seeker-specific sub-profile (lifestyle, job, preferences, location).

Enum values:
- `smokingHabit`: NEVER, SOMETIMES, REGULARLY
- `drinkingHabit`: NEVER, SOMETIMES, REGULARLY
- `foodHabit`: VEGETARIAN, NON_VEGETARIAN, VEGAN, EGGETARIAN
- `maritalStatus`: SINGLE, MARRIED, DIVORCED, WIDOWED
- `petHabit`: HAS_PETS, NO_PETS, LOVES_PETS

Returns a pre-signed S3 upload URL for the KYC document (e.g., Aadhar card front).

Query params:
- `fileName`: Name of the file to upload
- `type`: `document` (for govt ID) or `selfie` (for face photo)

After getting the URL, upload the file directly to S3 using a PUT request (not via this API). Then provide the final S3 URL in Complete KYC.

exec: pm.test('Got KYC document upload URL', function () {

request.description:
Returns a pre-signed S3 upload URL for the KYC selfie photo.

After getting the URL, upload the file directly to S3 using a PUT request. Then use the final `fileUrl` in Complete KYC.

exec: pm.test('Got KYC selfie upload URL', function () {

request.description:
Submits the KYC data (document type + S3 URLs) to complete identity verification.

`documentType` accepted values: `AADHAR_CARD`, `PAN_CARD`, `DRIVING_LICENSE`, `PASSPORT`.

Prerequisite: Complete steps 4 & 5 (Get KYC Upload URLs) and upload files to S3 first.
Registers the user as an OWNER role. Must be called after KYC. No request body required — the userId is in the path.
Returns the full role history of the user, including timestamps of when each role was added or switched.

item.name: Listings
item.description:
Manage property listings posted by owners. Supports publishing, draft saving, updating, deactivating, and fetching listings.

All endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`.

request.description:
Publishes a new property listing. All required fields must be provided.

Key enum values:
- `placeType`: APARTMENT, INDEPENDENT_HOUSE, PG, HOSTEL, VILLA
- `roomType`: PRIVATE_ROOM, SHARED_ROOM, ENTIRE_PLACE
- `furnishingStatus`: FULLY_FURNISHED, SEMI_FURNISHED, UNFURNISHED
- `propertyOnFloor`: GROUND, FIRST, SECOND, ... PENTHOUSE
- `floorPlan`: 1RK, 1BHK, 2BHK, 3BHK, 4BHK
- `bathroomType`: ATTACHED, SHARED, COMMON
- `occupantType`: FAMILY, BACHELORS, ROOMMATES, ANY
- `genderPreference`: MALE, FEMALE, ANY
- `noticePeriod`: 1_MONTH, 2_MONTHS, 3_MONTHS

body.raw:
{
  "ownerId": "{{ownerId}}",
  "propertyName": "Sunrise Apartments",
  "placeType": "APARTMENT",
  "roomType": "PRIVATE_ROOM",
  "availableFrom": "2026-04-01",
  "furnishingStatus": "SEMI_FURNISHED",
  "totalFloors": 6,
  "propertyOnFloor": "FIRST",
  "floorPlan": "2BHK",
  "floorType": "SINGLE",
  "balconyAvailable": true,
  "ageOfProperty": "0_1_YEARS",
  "carParking": 1,
  "bikeParking": 2,
  "latitude": 12.9716,
  "longitude": 77.5946,
  "showPreciseLocation": true,
  "country": "India",
  "flatHouseDetails": "Flat 4B",
  "streetAddress": "80 Feet Road",
  "landmark": "Near Forum Mall",
  "district": "Koramangala",
  "pinCode": "560034",
  "city": "Bangalore",
  "bathroomType": "ATTACHED",
  "occupantType": "ROOMMATES",
  "lights": 4,
  "fans": 3,
  "ac": 1,
  "tv": 1,
  "beds": 2,
  "wardrobes": 2,
  "geysers": 1,
  "amenities": ["Wifi", "Washing Machine", "Stove"],
  "customAmenities": ["Water Purifier"],
  "description": "Spacious 2BHK with good ventilation.",
  "petsAllowed": false,
  "drinkingAllowed": true,
  "smokingAllowed": false,
  "powerBackup": true,
  "photos": [
    "https://s3.amazonaws.com/flatmate-photos/listings/listing_001/photo_1.jpg",
    "https://s3.amazonaws.com/flatmate-photos/listings/listing_001/photo_2.jpg",
    "https://s3.amazonaws.com/flatmate-photos/listings/listing_001/photo_3.jpg",
    "https://s3.amazonaws.com/flatmate-photos/listings/listing_001/photo_4.jpg",
    "https://s3.amazonaws.com/flatmate-photos/listings/listing_001/photo_5.jpg"
  ],
  "rent": 15000,
  "minRent": 14000,
  "maxRent": 17000,
  "noticePeriod": "2_MONTHS",
  "roomsAvailable": 1,
  "genderPreference": "ANY",
  "occupancy": 2
}

request.description: Saves a partial/incomplete listing as a draft. Only a minimal set of fields is required. Useful for multi-step listing creation on mobile.
Updates an existing listing by its ID. Only include the fields you want to update (partial update).
Returns all active listings. Supports optional pagination or filter query params if available on the server.
Fetches full details of a single listing by its unique `listingId`.

exec: pm.test('Get listing by ID - 200 OK', function () {

request.description: Returns all listings (active and draft) belonging to a specific owner, identified by `ownerId`.

exec: pm.test('Get listings by owner - 200 OK', function () {

request.description: Deactivates (soft-deletes) a listing by ID. The listing will no longer appear in search results but is retained in the database.

Returns a pre-signed S3 upload URL for a listing photo.

After receiving the URL, perform a PUT request directly to S3 with the image binary. Use the returned `fileUrl` in the `photos` array when publishing/updating the listing.

exec: pm.test('Got listing photo upload URL - 200 OK', function () {

item.name: Location
item.description:
Geo-spatial endpoints for indexing listing coordinates and discovering nearby listings with filters.

All endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`.

request.description:
Indexes a listing's geographic coordinates into the location search engine (e.g., Redis geospatial index). Must be called after publishing a listing to make it discoverable via nearby search.

Usually called automatically by the backend after listing creation.

Returns listings within the given radius of the provided lat/lng coordinates, filtered by rent range, gender preference, and minimum available rooms.

Query params:
- `lat` / `lng`: Center coordinates
- `radius`: Distance in kilometers
- `minRent` / `maxRent`: Rent range filter
- `genderPreference`: MALE, FEMALE, ANY
- `minRooms`: Minimum number of available rooms

item.name: Swipes & Matches
item.description:
Tinder-style swipe and match flow between seekers and listings.

- Seeker swipes RIGHT on a listing → interest is recorded
- Owner reviews interests → approves a seeker → match created
- Both parties can then chat

All endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`.

request.description:
Records a swipe action by the seeker on a listing.

`type` values:
- `RIGHT`: Interested (like)
- `LEFT`: Not interested (pass)
Returns all seekers who swiped RIGHT on a specific listing. Owner uses this to review interested profiles before approving a match.

Owner approves a seeker's interest, creating a mutual match. After approval, a chat session is automatically opened between the two parties.

Prerequisite: The seeker must have swiped RIGHT on the owner's listing.
Returns all approved matches for the given user (either as seeker or owner). Each match entry includes the matched party's profile summary and chat status.

item.name: Chat
item.description:
REST endpoints for chat management. Real-time messaging is handled via WebSocket (not covered here).

Endpoints:
- Mark messages as read
- Fetch pre-signed upload URL for media (images, voice notes)
- Retrieve chat history by matchId

All endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`.

request.description:
Marks all unread messages in a chat session as read, from the perspective of the requesting role.

`role` values: `SEEKER` or `OWNER`.

exec: pm.test('Chat marked as read - 200 OK', function () {

request.description:
Returns a pre-signed S3 URL for uploading chat media (images, voice notes, etc.).

Supported file types: `.jpg`, `.png`, `.m4a`, `.mp4`, `.pdf`.

After uploading to S3 via PUT, send the `fileUrl` as a message payload over WebSocket.

query.description: Name of the media file to upload

exec: pm.test('Got chat media upload URL - 200 OK', function () {

request.description:
Returns the full message history for a match/chat session, identified by `matchId`.

Messages are returned in chronological order. Includes message type (TEXT, IMAGE, VOICE), sender role, timestamp, and read status.

item.name: Notifications
item.description:
Push notification management using Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM).

- Save/update device FCM token on login or app foreground
- Send a push notification to any user
- Fetch the notification inbox (in-app bell icon)

All endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`.

request.description:
Registers or updates the FCM push notification token for the user's device. Call this on every app launch or when the FCM token refreshes.

Replace `<FCM_DEVICE_TOKEN>` with the actual token obtained from the Firebase SDK.

Sends a push notification to the specified user via FCM. Use this for server-triggered events like new interests, matches, or chat messages.

Note: This endpoint is typically called internally by the backend (e.g., on match approval). It is exposed here for admin/testing purposes.

Returns the in-app notification inbox for a user (bell icon feed). Includes notifications about new interests, match approvals, and chat activity.

Notifications are returned in reverse chronological order (newest first).
